text: Hard to argue against that, especially when you add in lack of major injuries and longevity. The dude is literally a 1 of 1 athlete.
label: hot_take

text: I think LeBron's greatness has come in three distinct parts. First, his extraordinary athleticism, which in his prime almost no one could match. Later, his understanding of the game, with a mastery of in-game tactics and series-wide strategy that again very few have equaled. And last, his incredible longevity to still play at an all-star level into his 40s, which no one has ever done before. Any one of these three would have made for a great career, and any two would lock up a Hall of Fame selection.
label: structured_analysis

text: A big part of it is how coordinated and controlled he was. Lebron basically has perfect jumping and landing mechanics. Watch how he lands on all of these chase down blocks and dunks in traffic. His feet are almost always shoulder width apart and his knees are slightly bent. If he does land on one leg, it's never at a weird angle or with a straight leg. He also basically never lands on a foot. Compare that to someone like Rose or Ja Morant, and it's easy to see why they weren't durable and Lebron was.
label: structured_analysis

text: Any time the topic of his legendary athleticism comes up I just show them this 10 second clip. People who never watched him during the first few years really don't understand. notes: Edge case (emotional_reaction vs structured_analysis): cites concrete examples (48-minute games, full-court toss) but the dominant thrust is insistence/awe ("people don't understand, it was insane") rather than a built-out argument, so emotional_reaction

text: People always talk about LeBron being robbed of DPOY in 2012-13, but he was actually already second in DPOY voting in 2008-09, losing only to Dwight who was undoubtedly one of the great defensive players of the last 25 years or so. It was in Miami when he got big enough to truly guard the 5 and thus become genuinely capable of guarding 1-5 but he was already absurdly versatile in his first Cavs stint and was the best help defender/roamer in the league.
